questions.question: What holds the landing gear in the retracted (UP) position versus the extended (DOWN) position?

options.text: UP: mechanical uplocks only; DOWN: hydraulic pressure only
UP: hydraulic pressure only (no mechanical uplocks); DOWN: hydraulic pressure, downlock hooks, and spring pressure
UP: downlock hooks and springs; DOWN: hydraulic pressure only
UP: hydraulic pressure and mechanical uplocks; DOWN: gravity and downlock hooks

questions.explanation: The gear is held UP by hydraulic pressure only -- there are no mechanical uplocks. The gear is held DOWN by three independent methods: hydraulic pressure, downlock hooks, and spring pressure. This is a safety feature because a hydraulic failure will cause the gear to want to extend.

questions.question: What additional functions does the right main gear squat switch perform beyond preventing gear retraction on the ground?

options.text: It activates the stall warning system and starts the fuel totalizer
It arms the gear warning horn and activates the nose wheel steering
It de-rates the stall warning heat to 50% and starts the G1000 time counter
It disables the autopilot and activates the ground spoilers

questions.explanation: The right main gear squat switch has two additional functions beyond preventing ground retraction: it de-rates the stall warning heat to 50% when on the ground, and it starts the time counter on the G1000 (similar to tach time recording).

questions.question: If a gear leg does not lock down after emergency extension, what should the pilot do?

options.text: Cycle the gear handle UP and then DOWN again to retry
Increase airspeed above 152 KIAS to force the gear into position
Pump the emergency extension lever repeatedly to build hydraulic pressure
Reduce airspeed below 110 KIAS and apply moderate yawing and pitching motions

questions.explanation: If a gear leg will not lock after emergency extension, the pilot should reduce airspeed below 110 KIAS and apply moderate yawing and pitching motions. These maneuvers use inertia to help nudge the stubborn gear leg into the locked position.

questions.question: Under what conditions can the gear warning mute button NOT silence the horn?

options.text: When the aircraft is above 10,000 feet MSL
When BOTH power levers are below 20% or the flaps are in the landing position
When only one engine is operating
When the gear handle is in the DOWN position

questions.explanation: The gear warning mute button will NOT function if both engines are below 20% power or if the flaps are set to the landing position. This ensures the pilot always receives a warning during the most critical configurations.

questions.question: What are the correct damper strut extension measurements during preflight for the main gear and nose gear?

options.text: Main gear: 5.9 inches; Nose gear: 1.6 inches
Main gear: 3.0 inches; Nose gear: 3.0 inches
Main gear: 1.6 inches; Nose gear: 5.9 inches
Main gear: 2.5 inches; Nose gear: 4.0 inches

questions.explanation: During preflight, the main gear damper strut should be extended 1.6 inches, and the nose gear strut should be extended 5.9 inches. If either measurement is off, the shock absorber may need servicing.
